name: Ben Al-Jeri(LL0)
text_appearance:
Ben is typically seen wearing a somewhat bulky helmet. Majority of this bulk comes from the array of sensors and displays he used during his days piloting a Death's Head, this is in addition to his cybernetics, acting as a system of redundancy should either fail and as protection when conducting a Rodeo. His helmet also acts as a projector for his NHP named K.D.D.

K.D.D is often seen floating around and about beside Ben, either playfully recounting their experiences together or seeming like a vigilant guardian when both are out and about outside of engagements.
==========================================
Nowadays, he is often seen wearing a stealth hardsuit that is form fitting. At first, it was obvious he wasnt comfortable in it, he's gotten familiar with it as time goes by but he's still getting used to it
history:
Ben Al-Jeri grew up in the narrow corridors of a Far-field colony ship. Stopping over at new planets untouched by ThirdComm and hearing unfamiliar languages and living with new cultures. As a child, Ben spent long hours watching through the ship's viewports when in transit, it was during those quiet years that an NHP named K.D.D. first became part of his life. She chose a calm, feminine voice and spoke with steady patience, guiding him through long nights and grounding him when curiosity or anxiety pulled him too far into his own thoughts.

In adulthood, since Ben and his family had been a part of an FFT, Ben made the decision to join Union's Navy to further uphold the Pillars and nurture worlds and cultures like he and his family have always done in the past. NHP K.D.D. sticks around with Ben, however, unlike most NHPs, K.D.D plays a far simpler role in Ben's life as a guide or anchor when things get too shaky for Ben. She does not enter combat with Ben for now.

Fresh out of the academy, Ben is anxious and excited to be aboard the UNS-CV 'Rio Grande' along with his squad mates whom graduated alongside Ben. They're about to take undertake their first mission in fresh uniforms and shiny mechs.

Before Ben finally settles in his bunk to rest before the big day, he hears a voice, not of K.D.D or his squad mates. "...the man who passed the torch..."

He jolts up, but nothing and no one spoke, his squad is asleep, and K.D.D. is undergoing a cycle.

=========================================================

After gaining enough experience and showcasing his abilities in combat, Ben acquires an SSC Death's Head. Over the next months to pass, Ben trains and excels with a dedicated long-range fire support frame like the Death's Head, furthering his reputation.

One day, when printing a backup chassis of the Death's Head, the printer glitches and shuts down for a brief moment before continuing normally. For a moment the lights flicker and then there it was, a Pegasus pattern chassis. Ben and K.D.D are taken aback by the event that unfolded before them, but upon closer inspection by the pair, it would seem it bears no ill-will towards the pair. For a time, Ben's pilots the Pegasus to further augment his capabilities on the battlefield.

The Pegasus introduced Ben to things that were not meant to behave logically. After the frame was fully operational, the Sisyphus-class NHP was installed. Sisyphus speaks with the tone of a well learned scholar, calmly explaining events as though the outcome of the battle were simply a lesson being demonstrated.

When the Autogun interrupts combat in seamless free bursts, Sisyphus observes calmly, “Sustained pressure accelerates collapse.”. When incoming fire streaks toward the cockpit, “That trajectory is incompatible with your survival.”, and the shell misses. Not narrowly, but inevitably.

The Pegasus also carries the Ushabti Omnigun. It is mounted like a weapon and cataloged as one, yet when Ben “fires” it nothing happens. There is no recoil, no flash, and no sound. An enemy frame simply gains a clean, impossible absence where armor used to be. The first time Klondike witnessed it over comms he thought his sensors had failed.

Klondike once asked, “Ben… what did you just fire?”. “I didn’t.” Ben answered.

Inside the cockpit, Sisyphus clarifies, “Correction applied.”.

Sometimes the cost of piloting a machine like this arrives without warning. A searing headache tears through Ben’s mind and he sees flashes that feel like memories of the future. A collapsing structure. A missed step. Klondike wounded somewhere he has never been. Sometimes those moments unfold exactly as he saw them. Sometimes they never occur at all. Ben eventually came to a quiet conclusion. He is not seeing the future. For just an instant, he is seeing several possible outcomes at once, and only one of them becomes real. Sisyphus never claims responsibility for these visions. K.D.D. quietly lowers the sensory feed, dims the cockpit lighting, and steadies his breathing when they happen.

“It’s just pain...” she tells him gently. “...not prophecy. You are here, not there.”.

However Ben isn’t sure. And that uncertainty frightens him more than inevitability ever did.

==================================================

After learning the truth of his existence, he found some hidden license under the name of his progenitor and acquired an Oleander frame. Him being a clone is the reason why he sees visions of the future that may or may not come to pass, it wasn't the Pegasus, it was something latent in him all along.

===================================================

"We got assigned to a Bag and Tag team to reclaim some stuff from a ship offline in near the Economic zone of a planet. All was as usual until we made contact with a Prime NHP, the handbook advices us to blow the entire thing to smithereens but when we activated the scanners to get a lay of the ship, we inadvertently drew the attention of the extrusion of the NHP to our location. We had to make a quick getaway into a service elevator and went down into Engineering."

"Found a survivor in here, seems to be the last of the IPS-N crew stationed on this ship, he's broken and we could only get cryptic ramblings from him, but we understood. We need to get to the Command and Control Center and cycle the ship to retake control from the NHP and lock it back into its casket."

"Great, not only do we need to deal with the NHP extrusion, we came across an Aunic Saboteur and our weapons aren't getting through it. Armor-piercing, Para-causal munitions, Fire. Nothing is going through. Just what the hell is this guy?"

Transcription: "Good hit! Take that thing down! What the hell? Everyone get back! Bac-" Transcription Ends...

...Pilot Ben Al-Jeri. Callsign N/A. Active frame: Napoleon - Dublin Mudslide...

...Status: KIA...

...Err...Err...Status: MIA...Err...Status...UNDEFINED

"I must apologize, this isn't supposed to happen. As compensation, I shall grant all of you a second chance. Turn around and walk towards the light and claim your reparation. I'm sure someone in your line of work would appreciate something like this. However, you will forget everything that happened on this mission when you walk into that light. Everyone deserves a second chance."

"Pre-mission log. We're here on an escort mission but we've got some time before we need to head in. We'll be requisitioning some cargo from a ship that crash landed from the Economic Zone lanes. In the meantime, while we wait for the go signal, I'll give my armaments a once over."

"Our liason just told us not to mess the ship and the cargo up again. What do they mean again? We just got here. None of my visions showed me this... that ship... the cargo... I saw this before but- could've sworn I just- ah nevermind. My head is probably acting up again. This is Ben Al-Jeri, Independent Lancer, Weapons Officer and Designated Marksman from the Neapolitan Navigator. Signing off for now."

====================================================

Today was a different day for Ben, his usual visions never showed themselves, it was as if he had been blinded to all the possibilities that could happen today. Alas, there was little time to ponder and worry. There were crazier things that are bound to happen today.

Ben and his squad were being transported through a peaceful city to head to a briefing, then a few moments later in the dropship, the squad hears something akin to a thump and then suddenly the skies are filled with fire and ember. The outside was nothing like the peaceful city they flew into, one of the squad members recognizes that what's happening outside has happened in the past. This city was once torn by war and conflict, and they just got transported through space and time.

Since Ben's visions suddenly stopped, he's been reacting to situations like any normal person would, unlike his uncanny ability to seemingly know what to do next. Brake, one of the lancers in his squad, notices this "slowing down" of Ben.

There was no time to think or wonder how or why this happened as out of nowhere, an EINS Lancer contacts the squad and demands they identify themselves. The EINS Lancer, callsign LabRat, then decides to escort our dropship after we identify as friendlies. Unfortunately, after a short while, the dropship gets locked on by several anti-air missiles. LabRat springs into action, firing off all their flares, overcharging their reactor to act as a massive heat sig to mask the dropship. LabRat gets hit and goes down, Ben hoping they eject, but nothing could be done. Shortly after losing altitude, LabRat collides with a building, erupting in flames and debris.

Soon after LabRat goes down, the dropship gets hit. The pilot asks if we will ever see each other again, Lawrence answers, but before she could finish, our pilot jettisons us out moments before the dropship is engulfed in hellfire.

Two allies taken away in such a short time, none of which were foreseen by Ben.

The squad lands on a rooftop and an NHP Scientist shows up, looking at his watch every other moment. The squad gets a sneaking feeling that they might be responsible for messing with the flow of time. They move alongside an NHP null box, sometime after a tense standoff, the scientist pulls out a detonator, but Brake manages to fire off their ZF4. Shortly after, Lawrence gets targeted by a sniper, but they are pushed aside just in time, unfortunately the NHP null box was the target of the sniper and the box opens.

An Eidolon appears from the box and the squad is forced to fight. Another Lancer contacts the squad, callsign Siege Break, informing the squad that they have a limited amount of time before he is forced to order an artillery barrage on the position of the Eidolon. After a long battle, the final blow came from Lawrence, apparently they were spoken to by the Eidolon and the Eidolon is not dead.

Ben then regains his visions and is able to foresee events once more and the squad is then returned to their time of origin on the rooftop. The squad later learns from Lawrence that the Eidolon was a GRIMM-class named The Storyteller.
quirks: You keep having searing headaches during which you see brief flashes of what you’re pretty sure is the future. Sometimes these visions come to pass, sometimes they don’t.

systems.flavorDescription: By installing additional armor plating, redundant firing circuits, and enhanced insulation to magazines the longevity of a frame’s armaments can be greatly enhanced.

weapon.flavorDescription:
Much like GMS mechs, GMS weapons are reliable galactic standards, made using interchangeable parts and built to withstand almost any conditions. There are three lines currently in production.
The Type-III (T-3) line is made up of heavy weapons, ordnance, and other exotic, specialized, or massive weapons. A broad classification, the T-3 range includes conventional-kinetic anti-materiel rifles, super-rapid cycling pulse rifles, missile racks, cannons, and drone nexuses – “hive”style launchers that serve as miniature factory, hangar, and deployment systems for portable drones. The classification also includes the fearsome “segment knife”, a system that uses flash-printing to produce disposable edged weapons in a vicinity around the user.

bondPowers.name: Dabbler
bondPowers.description: Gain +2 to a trigger of your choice, up to a max of +6. You can change this bonus around at the end of each downtime.

bondPowers.name: Freesoul
bondPowers.description: You can escape from any restraint, shackle, hold, or prison cell without rolling. Nobody can hold, grab, or restrain you unless you let them. You can choose to conceal this ability if you wish.

bondPowers.name: Renaissance
bondPowers.description: At the start of any session, pick one of your triggers. You become an expert at that skill. When you roll it, roll two d20s and pick the highest for the final result. You must pick a new trigger each session, and can’t pick the same one twice in a row

bondPowers.name: Momentum
bondPowers.description:
When you survey a situation, ask one of the following questions and get a truthful answer from the GM:
  • How do I think I can get around this obstacle ?
  • What’ s the most direct way forward ?
  • Which way do I feel I should be moving ?
You or an ally of your choice gets + 1 Accuracy on their next skill check acting on the answer.

bondPowers.name: Long Road Home
bondPowers.prerequisite: You may only gain this power If you’ve taken four powers from this bond, including the Veteran Power.
bondPowers.description:
Name a location you’ve been in the last three days. You can make your way back there without rolling. The GM will determine the following about the journey there:
  • Was it a long or arduous journey?
  • Was the location dangerous, defended, or contested?
  • Were you being pursued, tracked, or hunted ?
For every “yes” answer, take 2 Stress; however, at the beginning of the next scene, you and up to ten other willing people arrive safely there.Don’t worry about playing out the journey(just ‘cut’ to the next scene as you are arriving) and work backwards to figure out how you got there.

bondAnswers: A star map given to you long ago by an old friend.
Deep into the molten core of a supernova.
minorIdeal: I beheld a unique, interesting, or beautiful sight.

weapons.flavorDescription: Light signature weapons might be oversized revolvers, braces of pistols, and submachine guns.
Light signature weapons might be oversized revolvers, braces of pistols, and submachine guns.

gear.flavorDescription: Dataplating is a general term for any comm-linked jewelry, subdermal netting, wearable jaw, brow, or maxillary plates, etc., that allows subvocal communication and persistent heads-up and augmented reality displays without wearing a helm. Dataplates can quickly translate nearly any language, and allow users to communicate with each other all but silently.
These clamps attach easily to any metal surface, enhancing maneuverability in zero-g environments or when repairing mechs. They can be carried or fitted to boots.
